skill.name: template-skill
skill.description: A starter template for drafting custom AI skill instructions and marketplace-ready skill metadata.
skill.summary: Create a basic structure for a reusable AI skill.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: The two duplicate high static findings at SKILL.md:3 are false positives. That line is a placeholder description, not cryptographic code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, filesystem access, script execution, environment access, or external commands were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer reported two duplicate weak cryptographic algorithm findings at SKILL.md:3. The line contains only the skill description placeholder and does not call or recommend any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contains only frontmatter description text. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm name, code path, or operational instruction to support the static finding.

content.value_statement: Teams need a consistent starting point when writing AI skills. This template gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a simple structure for skill metadata and instructions.
actual_capabilities: Provides a named SKILL.md frontmatter block for a custom skill.
Defines where a skill description should be written.
Includes a clear placeholder for adding operational instructions.
Works as a minimal starter file for marketplace skill authors.
Supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as declared compatible tools.
limitations: Does not include complete task instructions.
Does not provide scripts, helper files, or examples.
Does not automate validation or publishing steps.
Requires the author to replace all placeholder text before use.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Skill
use_cases.description: Start from a minimal SKILL.md file before adding workflow instructions and examples.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Give contributors a consistent metadata and instruction layout for new marketplace submissions.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Authoring
use_cases.description: Show new team members the basic fields expected in a custom AI skill.

prompt_templates.title: Fill the Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill template to draft clear instructions for a skill that helps with my chosen task.

prompt_templates.title: Improve the Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite the skill description so it clearly states when Claude, Codex, or Claude Code should use this skill.

prompt_templates.title: Add Workflow Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Expand the instruction section with step-by-step guidance, expected inputs, and user-facing output rules.

prompt_templates.title: Review for Publication
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this completed skill for unclear instructions, missing limitations, security concerns, and marketplace readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for reviewing product requirement documents.
output: A clear skill name and description.
Instruction sections for review scope, evidence gathering, and output format.
Limitations that explain what the skill cannot verify.

output_examples.input: Improve my draft skill before submission.
output: A short list of missing safety and quality checks.

best_practices: Replace the placeholder description with a precise usage trigger.
Add concrete steps that match the workflow the skill should support.
Document limitations and safety boundaries before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not publish the template with placeholder text still present.
Do not add instructions that override system or developer policies.
Do not claim capabilities that are not implemented in the skill files.

faq.question: Is this a complete skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a starter template that needs real instructions before production use.

faq.question: Which tools can use this template?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it run scripts or commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains only markdown frontmatter and a placeholder heading.

faq.question: Why were static security findings dismissed?
faq.answer: The findings pointed to a description placeholder, not cryptographic code or a risky instruction.

faq.question: What should authors add first?
faq.answer: Authors should replace the description and add clear instructions for when and how to use the skill.

faq.question: Can this be submitted as-is?
faq.answer: It can be published safely, but it has little practical value until the placeholders are replaced.
